assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response clearly states spot VMs can be preempted during upgrade process and mentions combined disruption from both upgrades and spot preemptions

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explicitly mention that spot workloads are already designed for interruption, making them suitable for upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response shows maxSurge: 2 with comment 'Higher surge for spot pools' in the YAML configuration

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends maxUnavailable: 0 with comment 'Avoid unavailable nodes during upgrade', which contradicts this expectation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Verify spot instance availability in your zones' and 'spot instance availability' as considerations

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response provides PDB configuration example and mentions 'Review and update PDBs for critical applications'

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response shows YAML configuration but doesn't provide the actual gcloud command to configure surge settings

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends upgrading on-demand nodes first, then spot nodes, which is the opposite of this expectation
